header.label: Stream Analytics ·
header.headline: Science 42.6% — 3.03 Cr students in Class 11–12
header.sub: Humanities holds a marginal lead in enrollment, with Science nearly at parity. Humanities remains female-majority (54.6%), while Commerce accounts for 14.7% of Class 11–12 students. Lab availability is measured across 3.0 L secondary schools.

cards.label: Science Stream
cards.note: Science accounts for 42.6% of Class 11–12 enrollment. Girls represent 47.1% of Science students, and 50.4% of Science enrollment is rural.

cards.label: Humanities Stream
cards.note: Humanities accounts for 42.7% of Class 11–12 enrollment. Girls represent 54.6% of Humanities students, and 67.4% of Humanities enrollment is rural.

cards.label: Commerce Stream
cards.note: Commerce accounts for 14.7% of Class 11–12 enrollment. Girls represent 45.6% of Commerce students, and 68.9% of Commerce enrollment is urban.

cards.label: Lab Infrastructure
cards.headlineNote: Chemistry Lab availability in secondary schools
cards.barLabel: 0.7 L secondary schools have Chemistry Lab
cards.note: Chemistry labs are available in 23.8% of secondary schools; Physics labs in 23.7%; Biology labs in 23.2%.

2023-24.chartStreamSource: At the national level, Humanities and Science are nearly at parity in Class 11–12 enrollment. Rural enrollment is led by Humanities (52.5%), while urban enrollment is led by Science (46.8%).
2023-24.chartStreamNote: Humanities 42.7%, Science 42.6%, Commerce 14.7%. Total Class 11–12 enrollment: 303.5 L students.
2023-24.chartGenderSource: Humanities remains the only female-majority stream. Science and Commerce continue to have a male majority, though Science is closer to parity than Commerce.
2023-24.chartGenderNote: Female share by stream: Humanities 54.6%, Science 47.1%, Commerce 45.6%.
2023-24.chartLabSource: Among secondary schools, Chemistry, Physics, and Biology labs have the widest coverage at roughly one-quarter of schools. Computer labs are less widespread, and specialist labs remain limited.
2023-24.chartLabNote: Chemistry 23.8%, Physics 23.7%, Biology 23.2%, Computer 19.7% of secondary schools.

insight1.label: Humanities — Marginally Largest Stream by Enrollment

insight2.label: Chemistry Lab — Widest Coverage Among Secondary-School Labs

header.label: Stream Analytics ·
header.headline: Science 44.0% — 3.14 Cr students in Class 11–12
header.sub: Science becomes the largest stream by enrollment. Humanities remains female-majority (55.4%), while Commerce accounts for 14.5% of Class 11–12 students. Lab availability is measured across 3.1 L secondary schools.

cards.label: Science Stream
cards.note: Science accounts for 44.0% of Class 11–12 enrollment. Girls represent 47.8% of Science students, and 51.6% of Science enrollment is rural.

cards.label: Humanities Stream
cards.note: Humanities accounts for 41.4% of Class 11–12 enrollment. Girls represent 55.4% of Humanities students, and 68.0% of Humanities enrollment is rural.

cards.label: Commerce Stream
cards.note: Commerce accounts for 14.5% of Class 11–12 enrollment. Girls represent 45.8% of Commerce students, and 68.9% of Commerce enrollment is urban.

cards.label: Lab Infrastructure
cards.headlineNote: Chemistry Lab availability in secondary schools
cards.barLabel: 0.8 L secondary schools have Chemistry Lab
cards.note: Chemistry labs are available in 25.7% of secondary schools; Physics labs in 25.7%; Biology labs in 25.1%.

2024-25.chartStreamSource: Science becomes the largest stream nationally. Rural Class 11–12 enrollment remains led by Humanities (50.8%), while urban enrollment continues to be led by Science (47.8%).
2024-25.chartStreamNote: Science 44.0%, Humanities 41.4%, Commerce 14.5%. Total Class 11–12 enrollment: 3.145 Cr students.
2024-25.chartGenderSource: Humanities remains the only female-majority stream. Science and Commerce continue to have a male majority, though Science is closer to parity than Commerce.
2024-25.chartGenderNote: Female share by stream: Humanities 55.4%, Science 47.8%, Commerce 45.8%.
2024-25.chartLabSource: Among secondary schools, Chemistry, Physics, and Biology labs have the widest coverage at roughly one-quarter of schools. Computer labs remain below core science-lab coverage, and specialist labs continue to be limited.
2024-25.chartLabNote: Chemistry 25.7%, Physics 25.7%, Biology 25.1%, Computer 22.8% of secondary schools.

insight2.label: Chemistry Lab — Widest Coverage Among Secondary-School Labs
